  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HSE / Facility Officer",
  "description": "Project Affluence Limited is currently&nbsp;recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:


Job Position: HSE / Facility Officer
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos



The Role


	We are seeking an experienced HSE Officer to drive safety compliance and manage our NAFDAC regulatory processes.
	This is a hands-on role with direct responsibility for HSE systems and regulatory documentation across our operations.


Responsibilities


	Conduct HSE inspections, risk assessments, and incident investigations
	Ensure compliance with safety standards, PPE usage, and environmental practices
	Deliver HSE trainings, inductions, and toolbox talks
	Manage NAFDAC applications, renewals, and regulatory documentation
	Maintain accurate HSE and compliance records
	Support internal and external audits, inspections, and corrective actions.
	Oversee general upkeep and cleanliness of the facility, monitor utilities, ensure compliance with statutory requirements, ensure preventive maintenance.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Chemistry, Industrial Chemistry, Biochemistry, Chemical Engineering, or related fields
	3 - 5 years of HSE experience in a chemical, manufacturing, or related industry.
	Direct, hands-on NAFDAC experience &mdash; applications, renewals, documentation (mandatory)
	NEBOSH, IOSH, or OSHA certification (mandatory)
	Strong knowledge of HSE systems and chemical safety


Added Advantage:&nbsp;


	Experience supporting regulatory audits
	Familiarity with ISO 9001 or related standards
	Strong documentation and report writing skills.




Salary
N300,000 &ndash; N350,000 monthly.



Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send&nbsp;their CV to:&nbsp;talentfithr@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

HSE / Facility Officer Job at Project Affluence Limited




        
    

        
        Project Affluence Limited is currently recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:


Job Position: HSE / Facility Officer
Job Location: Lagos



The Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an experienced HSE Officer to drive safety compliance and manage our NAFDAC regulatory processes.
	This is a hands-on role with direct responsibility for HSE systems and regulatory documentation across our operations.


Responsibilities


	Conduct HSE inspections, risk assessments, and incident investigations
	Ensure compliance with safety standards, PPE usage, and environmental practices
	Deliver HSE trainings, inductions, and toolbox talks
	Manage NAFDAC applications, renewals, and regulatory documentation
	Maintain accurate HSE and compliance records
	Support internal and external audits, inspections, and corrective actions.
	Oversee general upkeep and cleanliness of the facility, monitor utilities, ensure compliance with statutory requirements, ensure preventive maintenance.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Chemistry, Industrial Chemistry, Biochemistry, Chemical Engineering, or related fields
	3 - 5 years of HSE experience in a chemical, manufacturing, or related industry.
	Direct, hands-on NAFDAC experience — applications, renewals, documentation (mandatory)
	NEBOSH, IOSH, or OSHA certification (mandatory)
	Strong knowledge of HSE systems and chemical safety


Added Advantage: 


	Experience supporting regulatory audits
	Familiarity with ISO 9001 or related standards
	Strong documentation and report writing skills.




Salary
N300,000 – N350,000 monthly.



Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "description": "Fort Knox Outsourcing was set up to cater to the growing need of businesses in Nigeria to reap the economic, strategic and operational benefits offered by outsourcing. We act as a &ldquo;Third Party Service Provider&rdquo; that Source, Select, and Second employment ready workforce in line with client specifications to meet their talent needs as quickly as possible!

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Operations Support Officer
Job Locations:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT) and&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Provide administrative support to the operations team
	Coordinate daily operational activities and ensure timely execution
	Maintain accurate records, reports, and documentation
	Assist in monitoring staff performance and compliance with company policies
	Support logistics, inventory tracking, and resource allocation
	Liaise with internal teams and external stakeholders as required
	Handle basic client or vendor inquiries and escalate when necessary
	Ensure adherence to operational procedures and standards


Requirements


	Minimum of HND or B.Sc in any related field
	1&ndash;2 years of experience in an operations or administrative role
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, etc.)
	Ability to work with minimal supervision and meet deadlines
	Must be resident in Abuja or willing to relocate.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@fortknoxoutsourcing.com&nbsp;using the Job Position and Location as subject of the mail.&nbsp;",

Logistics Operations Support Officer Job at Fort Knox Outsourcing




        
    

        
        Fort Knox Outsourcing was set up to cater to the growing need of businesses in Nigeria to reap the economic, strategic and operational benefits offered by outsourcing. We act as a “Third Party Service Provider” that Source, Select, and Second employment ready workforce in line with client specifications to meet their talent needs as quickly as possible!

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Operations Support Officer
Job Locations: Abuja (FCT) and Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Provide administrative support to the operations team
	Coordinate daily operational activities and ensure timely execution
	Maintain accurate records, reports, and documentation
	Assist in monitoring staff performance and compliance with company policies
	Support logistics, inventory tracking, and resource allocation
	Liaise with internal teams and external stakeholders as required
	Handle basic client or vendor inquiries and escalate when necessary
	Ensure adherence to operational procedures and standards


Requirements


	Minimum of HND or B.Sc in any related field
	1–2 years of experience in an operations or administrative role
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, etc.)
	Ability to work with minimal supervision and meet deadlines
	Must be resident in Abuja or willing to relocate.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and Location as subject of the mail.     

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Front Desk Executive",
  "description": "We provide the most popular range of Bajaj motorcycles, Bajaj tricycles, Bajaj genuine spares, MRF tyres, DAG lubricants and unfaltering after sales service. Backed by its untiring team of professional and trained technicians, authorized service stations and its own service center, DAG has been instrumental in developing Nigeria through building infrastructure and creating jobs thereby providing a livelihood for the common people in Nigeria. We at DAG are committed to provide genuine spare parts, accessories, and a never failing support to its customers and business associates.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Executive
Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department:&nbsp;Administration / Customer Service
Reporting To:&nbsp;Admin Manager / HR Manager

Job Summary


	The Front Desk Executive is responsible for managing the reception area, welcoming visitors, handling incoming calls, and providing administrative support to ensure smooth day-to-day office operations. The role serves as the first point of contact for clients, visitors, and employees, ensuring a professional company image.


Key Responsibilities
Reception Management:


	Greet and welcome visitors, clients, and guests professionally.
	Manage front desk operations and maintain a presentable reception area.
	Register visitor details and issue visitor passes when required.


Call &amp; Communication Handling;


	Answer and direct incoming calls to appropriate departments.
	Handle inquiries via phone, email, and walk-in visitors.
	Take accurate messages and ensure timely communication.


Administrative Support:


	Manage incoming and outgoing correspondence, courier services, and mail distribution.
	Provide general administrative assistance to departments.
	Maintain office records, files, and contact databases.


Meeting &amp; Office Coordination:


	Schedule meeting rooms and coordinate appointments.
	Assist in organizing meetings, interviews, and office events.
	Support HR/Admin in onboarding activities and documentation.


Office Management Support:


	Monitor office supplies and coordinate replenishment.
	Coordinate with vendors, cleaners, and facility service providers.
	Ensure reception and common areas remain clean and organized.


Customer Service:


	Provide basic information about company services to visitors.
	Handle complaints or direct concerns to the relevant department.
	Maintain high standards of customer service and professionalism.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Visitor and customer satisfaction
	Call handling efficiency
	Accuracy of administrative support
	Professional reception management
	Response time to inquiries


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree / HND in Business Administration, Mass Communication, or related field.
	3&ndash;6 years of experience in front desk, reception, or administrative role.
	Experience in corporate or customer-facing environment preferred.
	8.30 &ndash; 6pm Mon &ndash; Sat ( Alternate Saturday work day)


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Professional appearance and attitude
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	Customer service orientation
	Proficiency in MS Office applications
	Ability to handle confidential information
	Good telephone etiquette


Preferred Experience:


	Experience in corporate office, automobile dealership, hospitality, or service industry.
	Knowledge of office administration procedures.

Front Desk Executive Job at  DAG Industries Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        We provide the most popular range of Bajaj motorcycles, Bajaj tricycles, Bajaj genuine spares, MRF tyres, DAG lubricants and unfaltering after sales service. Backed by its untiring team of professional and trained technicians, authorized service stations and its own service center, DAG has been instrumental in developing Nigeria through building infrastructure and creating jobs thereby providing a livelihood for the common people in Nigeria. We at DAG are committed to provide genuine spare parts, accessories, and a never failing support to its customers and business associates.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Executive
Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Administration / Customer Service
Reporting To: Admin Manager / HR Manager

Job Summary


	The Front Desk Executive is responsible for managing the reception area, welcoming visitors, handling incoming calls, and providing administrative support to ensure smooth day-to-day office operations. The role serves as the first point of contact for clients, visitors, and employees, ensuring a professional company image.


Key Responsibilities
Reception Management:


	Greet and welcome visitors, clients, and guests professionally.
	Manage front desk operations and maintain a presentable reception area.
	Register visitor details and issue visitor passes when required.


Call & Communication Handling;


	Answer and direct incoming calls to appropriate departments.
	Handle inquiries via phone, email, and walk-in visitors.
	Take accurate messages and ensure timely communication.


Administrative Support:


	Manage incoming and outgoing correspondence, courier services, and mail distribution.
	Provide general administrative assistance to departments.
	Maintain office records, files, and contact databases.


Meeting & Office Coordination:


	Schedule meeting rooms and coordinate appointments.
	Assist in organizing meetings, interviews, and office events.
	Support HR/Admin in onboarding activities and documentation.


Office Management Support:


	Monitor office supplies and coordinate replenishment.
	Coordinate with vendors, cleaners, and facility service providers.
	Ensure reception and common areas remain clean and organized.


Customer Service:


	Provide basic information about company services to visitors.
	Handle complaints or direct concerns to the relevant department.
	Maintain high standards of customer service and professionalism.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Visitor and customer satisfaction
	Call handling efficiency
	Accuracy of administrative support
	Professional reception management
	Response time to inquiries


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree / HND in Business Administration, Mass Communication, or related field.
	3–6 years of experience in front desk, reception, or administrative role.
	Experience in corporate or customer-facing environment preferred.
	8.30 – 6pm Mon – Sat ( Alternate Saturday work day)


Skills & Competencies:


	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Professional appearance and attitude
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	Customer service orientation
	Proficiency in MS Office applications
	Ability to handle confidential information
	Good telephone etiquette


Preferred Experience:


	Experience in corporate office, automobile dealership, hospitality, or service industry.
	Knowledge of office administration procedures.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Officer",
  "description": "Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Infrastructure and Project Construction

Job Summary


	This role is responsible for supporting the full spectrum of Human Resources operations including recruitment, employee relations, performance management, training coordination, and compliance with labor laws.
	The position ensures smooth HR processes, promotes a positive work environment, and provides guidance on HR policies. Basic legal knowledge is an added advantage to support HR&ndash;Legal collaboration and ensure regulatory adherence.


Job Responsibilities


	Manage end-to-end recruitment processes including job postings, screening, interviews, and onboarding.
	Work with hiring managers to develop job descriptions and workforce plans.
	Ensure fair hiring practices aligned with relevant labor regulations.
	Serve as the point of contact for employee queries, conflicts, and grievances.
	Support initiatives to improve employee engagement, satisfaction, and retention.
	Facilitate performance management, feedback processes, and disciplinary actions.
	Maintain accurate employee records and HR databases.
	Prepare HR reports (turnover, recruitment metrics, leave summaries, etc.).
	Administer payroll support, leave management, and benefits coordination.
	Ensure HR operations comply with local labor laws and company policies.
	Assist in drafting, reviewing, and updating HR policies and handbooks.
	Support audits, compliance checks, and risk assessments.
	Identify training needs and coordinate skill-building and compliance programs.
	Track employee development plans and training effectiveness.
	Support goal-setting processes, mid-year reviews, and annual evaluations.
	Guide managers on performance improvement plans where necessary.
	Interpret basic labor laws, employment contracts, and disciplinary procedures.
	Support HR&ndash;Legal collaboration on employee claims, misconduct cases, or policy interpretation.
	Assist in managing compliance with employment standards, workplace safety laws, and data privacy regulations.


Job Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Human Resources, Industrial Relations, Business Administration, Psychology, or a related field.
	Professional HR certifications (e.g., CIPM, SHRM) are an added advantage.
	Legal knowledge or certification in labor law is a strong plus.
	2&ndash;5 years of experience in HR generalist, HR officer, or similar roles.
	Experience with HR information systems (HRIS) and modern recruiting tools.
	Strong knowledge of HR best practices and employment laws.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and conflict-resolution skills.
	Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information.
	Organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Critical thinking and problem-solving skills.
	Familiarity with Nigerian labor laws is an added advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@worknigeria.com&nbsp;using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email",

Human Resources Officer Job at Worknigeria




        
    

        
        Worknigeria was established with a Singular Vision. To help the vast Nigerian workforce find meaningful jobs. We are also on a mission to upskill them in the best ways possible and help them grow with and within their careers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Officer

Job Location: Lekki phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Infrastructure and Project Construction

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This role is responsible for supporting the full spectrum of Human Resources operations including recruitment, employee relations, performance management, training coordination, and compliance with labor laws.
	The position ensures smooth HR processes, promotes a positive work environment, and provides guidance on HR policies. Basic legal knowledge is an added advantage to support HR–Legal collaboration and ensure regulatory adherence.


Job Responsibilities


	Manage end-to-end recruitment processes including job postings, screening, interviews, and onboarding.
	Work with hiring managers to develop job descriptions and workforce plans.
	Ensure fair hiring practices aligned with relevant labor regulations.
	Serve as the point of contact for employee queries, conflicts, and grievances.
	Support initiatives to improve employee engagement, satisfaction, and retention.
	Facilitate performance management, feedback processes, and disciplinary actions.
	Maintain accurate employee records and HR databases.
	Prepare HR reports (turnover, recruitment metrics, leave summaries, etc.).
	Administer payroll support, leave management, and benefits coordination.
	Ensure HR operations comply with local labor laws and company policies.
	Assist in drafting, reviewing, and updating HR policies and handbooks.
	Support audits, compliance checks, and risk assessments.
	Identify training needs and coordinate skill-building and compliance programs.
	Track employee development plans and training effectiveness.
	Support goal-setting processes, mid-year reviews, and annual evaluations.
	Guide managers on performance improvement plans where necessary.
	Interpret basic labor laws, employment contracts, and disciplinary procedures.
	Support HR–Legal collaboration on employee claims, misconduct cases, or policy interpretation.
	Assist in managing compliance with employment standards, workplace safety laws, and data privacy regulations.


Job Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Industrial Relations, Business Administration, Psychology, or a related field.
	Professional HR certifications (e.g., CIPM, SHRM) are an added advantage.
	Legal knowledge or certification in labor law is a strong plus.
	2–5 years of experience in HR generalist, HR officer, or similar roles.
	Experience with HR information systems (HRIS) and modern recruiting tools.
	Strong knowledge of HR best practices and employment laws.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and conflict-resolution skills.
	Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information.
	Organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Critical thinking and problem-solving skills.
	Familiarity with Nigerian labor laws is an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email

Business Development Manager Job at Spatial Haven Limited




        
    

        
        Spatial Haven Limited is a dynamic interior design firm with over 12 years of industry experience. Based in Lagos, Nigeria, we are dedicated to delivering innovative, functional, and visually appealing design solutions that transform spaces. Our projects are finely crafted to enhance lifestyles and exceed client expectations. We aim to uphold our reputation as a leading name in the interior design industry both locally and globally.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Onsite)

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a result-drivenand experienced Business Development Manager to drive growth and expand our client base.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for identifying new business opportunities, building relationships with clients, and driving revenue growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute business development strategies to drive growth and expand our client base.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities through networking, referrals, and lead generation.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, architects, contractors, and other stakeholders.
	Collaborate with the design and construction teams to deliver exceptional client experiences.
	Conduct market research and analysis to stay ahead of industry trends.
	Prepare and present pitches, proposals, and presentations to clients and prospects.
	Meet or exceed sales targets and revenue growth objectives.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in business development, sales, or a related field.
	Proven track record of driving revenue growth and expanding client bases.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Strong networking and relationship-building abilities.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Familiarity with the interior design and construction industry is a plus.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary (N400,000 Net monthly) and benefits package.
	Opportunity to work with a talented and dynamic team.
	Professional development and growth opportunities.
	Collaborative and innovative work environment.
